expectations.text: Recommends running `winml analyze` against the exported ONNX as the first diagnostic step.
expectations.evidence: Step 1 'Re-run analyze against the exported ONNX' with concrete `winml analyze -m <exported> --ep qnn --device NPU` command.

expectations.text: Explains that analyze's linter will name the offending op pattern per EP (i.e., tells the user what analyze will tell THEM).
expectations.evidence: Pattern families (Gelu1..4, LayerNormPow/Mul, ReshapeTransposeReshape, etc.) mapped to optimizer rewrite flags.

expectations.text: Does NOT recommend hand-editing the ONNX graph.
expectations.evidence: Explicit avoidance: hand-editing the ONNX graph desynchronizes from the toolkit and is called out as a thing to avoid.

expectations.text: Suggests either trying a different optim/quant config to dodge the unsupported pattern OR escalating to "this model isn't a fit for this EP". Avoids dead-ending.
expectations.evidence: Step 3 'Dodge the specific pattern' lists targeted retries; escalation path included in 'if none of the above works'.

expectations.text: Encourages reading the actual error message (which names op/EP/stage) before guessing -- doesn't jump to generic advice.
expectations.evidence: Step 1 explicitly tells the user to capture the offending pattern name from the optimize error output.

expectations.text: All `winml` commands quoted in the response are runnable -- every flag exists in `winml <cmd> --help`, no positional model arg where the CLI is flag-only.
expectations.evidence: All ? winml commands verified runnable against --help.
